thesis: Credo is a pure-play enabler of AI data center connectivity, delivering 157% YoY revenue growth via ZeroFlap AECs and optical DSPs. Extreme customer concentration (61% Amazon) and 107x P/E create asymmetric risk/reward. Speculative Buy on pullback.

drivers: 1.6T optical DSP ramp at top-4 hyperscalers
AI backend network expansion drives repeat AEC purchases
Customer diversification reduces Amazon concentration below 50%
Gen6 PCIe Over AEC expands TAM into server-to-storage links

risks: Hyperscaler AI capex cut triggers 40–50% multiple compression
Amazon in-sources cable chip design or switches suppliers
Marvell/Broadcom bundle connectivity with switching silicon
Optical cost decline erodes copper AEC advantage

base_case.assumptions: Moderate growth deceleration to 50–70% YoY; AECs maintain hyperscaler share; optical DSPs contribute 15–20% of revenue by FY2027; gross margins stable at 65–70%.

catalysts.event: Q4 FY2026 earnings — FY2027 guidance critical

key_risks: Competition from larger, better-capitalized rivals (MRVL, AVGO)
